tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and imagine two different baskets of fruit. One represents hope, the other represents despair. Which basket are you choosing to focus on today?

two_step.reflect_question: How do you respond when facing difficult seasons of life?

options.response: Practice gratitude and seek God's perspective
Lean into community and spiritual support

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw two baskets - one with fresh, ripe fruit representing hope, and another with spoiled fruit representing despair. Decorate the 'hope' basket with bright colors.

reevaluation.question: How has this passage changed your view of difficult seasons?
options: I see challenges as opportunities for growth
God is present even in painful transitions
Maintaining faith matters more than circumstances

three_choice.question: Which symbol of divine judgment most resonates with you?

choices.expansion: Those who remain humble and trust God's plan even in difficult circumstances are ultimately preserved.
Persistent rebellion against divine guidance leads to complete spiritual and national deterioration.
Suffering can be a profound mechanism of spiritual purification and redirection.

socratic_nudge.question: If God uses adversity as a tool of transformation, how might your current challenges be secretly cultivating your spiritual growth?

moral_slider.question: To what extent do collective national sins determine individual spiritual outcomes?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of divine judgment shifted?
options: More nuanced view of God's mercy
Increased empathy for those experiencing hardship

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Judgment and Restoration
thematic_summaries.summary: God uses the Babylonian exile as a mechanism of judgment against Judah's persistent disobedience. The vision of good and bad figs represents God's discernment between those who will be refined and those who will be ultimately rejected.

thematic_summaries.title: Prophetic Symbolism of Restoration
thematic_summaries.summary: The good figs symbolize a remnant whom God will preserve, restore, and ultimately bring back to their land with spiritual renewal. This prophecy demonstrates God's redemptive plan even amid severe national punishment.

reevaluation.question: How does God's judgment in this chapter reflect His ultimate redemptive purposes?
options: Punishment is purely punitive and destructive
Discipline serves a restorative and transformative purpose
God arbitrarily selects who will be preserved
